courseTitle: Awareness of Learning Disability and Autism

questions.question: Which of the following best describes a learning disability?
options: A lifelong condition, present from before adulthood, that affects how a person understands information, learns new skills and copes independently
A mental illness that develops in adult life and can usually be cured
A temporary problem with reading and spelling
An injury that only affects physical movement

questions.question: How does a learning disability differ from a learning difficulty such as dyslexia?
options: A learning difficulty tends to affect specific skills like reading, while a learning disability affects wider understanding, learning and coping in daily life
They mean exactly the same thing
A learning difficulty is always more disabling than a learning disability
A learning disability only affects a person's spelling

questions.question: Which statement about a learning disability is correct?
options: It is not an illness and is not a mental illness
It is a type of mental illness
It is an infection that can be passed to others
It always disappears once a person reaches adulthood

questions.question: What is the correct status of this awareness course?
options: It is an introductory awareness course and does not replace the Oliver McGowan Mandatory Training on Learning Disability and Autism
Completing it means a person has completed the Oliver McGowan Mandatory Training
It is the legally mandated training required under the Health and Care Act 2022
It removes any need for further learning about autism

questions.question: Which statement about the Oliver McGowan Mandatory Training is correct?
options: It is delivered by an approved provider and co-delivered with people who have lived experience of learning disability and autism
It can be fully completed by reading a single awareness e-learning module like this one
It is optional guidance with no legal basis
It is only intended for senior managers

questions.question: Makaton is best described as:
options: A way of communicating that uses signs and symbols alongside speech
A prescription medicine used to calm behaviour
A medical diagnosis given in childhood
A legal document about a person's care

questions.question: What does 'diagnostic overshadowing' mean?
options: A person's physical or mental health symptoms are wrongly blamed on their learning disability or autism, so a treatable condition is missed
A person is given more than one diagnosis on the same day
A diagnosis is explained using easy-read materials
A person is diagnosed earlier than usual

questions.question: A man with a learning disability keeps holding his jaw and going off his food, but staff assume this is 'just part of his disability' and do not arrange a dental check. This is an example of:
options: Diagnostic overshadowing
A reasonable adjustment
Positive behaviour support
Masking

questions.question: Compared with the general population, people with a learning disability in the UK are more likely to:
options: Die earlier from causes that could often have been prevented or treated
Live significantly longer on average
Have identical health outcomes in every respect
Be unable to register with a GP

questions.question: What is the main aim of the LeDeR programme (Learning from Lives and Deaths)?
options: To learn from the deaths of people with a learning disability and autistic people in order to improve care
To decide which individual staff members should be disciplined
To decide who is eligible for treatment
To set medication doses

questions.question: Which statement best describes autism?
options: A lifelong difference in how a person experiences the world, communicates and processes sensory information
A mental illness that can be cured with medication
A short phase that children grow out of
A condition caused by poor parenting

questions.question: Which statement about autism and learning disability is correct?
options: Autism is not a learning disability, though some people are both autistic and have a learning disability
Every autistic person also has a learning disability
Autism and learning disability are exactly the same condition
An autistic person can never also have a learning disability

questions.question: Many autistic people prefer 'identity-first' language. In practice this means:
options: Using 'autistic person' rather than 'person with autism'
Avoiding any mention of autism
Always using 'person with autism'
Using only clinical terminology

questions.question: In relation to autism, 'masking' refers to:
options: Hiding or suppressing natural traits to fit in, which takes hidden effort and can be exhausting
Wearing a face covering for infection prevention
A refusal to communicate with others
The moment a person receives their diagnosis

questions.question: What is a helpful response when an autistic person is experiencing a meltdown or shutdown?
options: Reduce demands, give them space and time, and stay calm
Raise your voice so they pay attention
Insist they keep going with the activity
Treat it as deliberate misbehaviour that needs discipline

questions.question: Which statement about mental capacity is correct?
options: Having a learning disability or being autistic does not, on its own, mean a person lacks the capacity to make a decision
A person with a learning disability can never make their own decisions
Capacity is decided once and then applies to every decision for life
Autistic people automatically lack capacity to consent

questions.question: Annual health checks for people with a learning disability (aged 14 and over) are important because they:
options: Help find and treat health problems early that might otherwise be missed
Are only about checking a person's weight
Replace the need for any other medical care
Are used to decide if someone can keep their job

questions.question: Before helping someone with their personal care, the right approach is to:
options: Seek the person's consent and support them to make the decision in a way they understand
Proceed without asking because they have a learning disability
Ask a relative instead of the person every time
Assume they will always refuse and skip the care

questions.question: Autism is a type of learning disability.
options: True
False

questions.question: This awareness course replaces the Oliver McGowan Mandatory Training on Learning Disability and Autism.
options: True
False

questions.question: You should presume that a person has the capacity to make a decision unless it is shown otherwise.
options: True
False

questions.question: A meltdown is deliberate, attention-seeking behaviour that should be ignored.
options: True
False

questions.question: Restrictive practice should only be used as a last resort and should always be the least restrictive option.
options: True
False

questions.question: Select all that apply. Which of these can help you communicate well with a person with a learning disability or an autistic person?
options: Using Makaton signs and symbols
Providing easy-read information with pictures
Allowing extra time to process information and respond
Using long, complex sentences full of technical jargon

questions.question: Select all that apply. Which statements about autism are correct?
options: It is a lifelong difference, not an illness to be cured
Autistic people may experience sensory information very differently from others
A person can be both autistic and have a learning disability
Autism is a type of learning disability

questions.question: Select all that apply. Which of these support good, safe healthcare for people with a learning disability and autistic people?
options: A hospital or health passport describing their needs and how to support them
Annual health checks for those who are eligible
Making reasonable adjustments to how care is delivered
Assuming any new symptom is simply part of their disability

questions.question: Select all that apply. Which of these reflect a positive behaviour support approach?
options: Recognising that behaviour is often a form of communication
Looking for unmet needs, pain or distress behind the behaviour
Reaching for restrictive practices as the first response
Adjusting the environment to reduce stress and triggers

questions.question: Complete: ___ overshadowing is when a treatable illness is wrongly blamed on a person's learning disability or autism.
Complete: STOMP aims to stop the over-medication of people with a learning disability and autistic people with ___ medicines that are used to manage behaviour.
Complete: Under the Equality Act 2010, services have a legal duty to make reasonable ___ for disabled people.
